title: Yoshua Bengio: Home
excerpts:
Section Title: Home > A.M. Turing Award Recipient and most-cited AI researcher
Content:
**Yoshua Bengio** is Full Professor of Computer Science at [Université de Montreal](https://www.umontreal.ca/) , Co-President and Scientific Director of [LawZero](http://lawzero.org/) , as well as the Founder and Scientific Advisor of [Mila](http://mila.quebec/) . He also holds a Canada [CIFAR](https://cifar.ca/) AI Chair. Considered one of the world’s leaders in Artificial Intelligence and Deep Learning, he is the recipient of the 2018 A.M. Turing Award, considered to be the “Nobel Prize of computing.” He is the most cited computer scientist worldwide, and the most-cited living scientist across all fields (by total citations).

title: About Mila | Mila
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Founded by Professor Yoshua Bengio, Mila is now the world’s largest academic research cente...
Content:
Yoshua Bengio, a Quebec researcher who specializes in AI at the Université de Montréal, is one of the precursors of deep learning. In 1993, he founded a research lab to establish a community of high calibre researchers who share the same ethical vision for the development of AI and a common interest in the advancement of technology with extraordinary potential for society.
In 2017, Mila expands the scope of its activities through a collaboration between Université de Montréal and McGill University, working closely with Polytechnique Montréal and HEC Montréal. Today, Mila brings together a community of professors, student researchers (master’s, doctorate, postdoctorate, interns, visitors), students (professional master’s, specialized graduate degree), industry partners and startups working in AI.

Zhilin Yang, Peng Qi, Saizheng Zhang, Yoshua Bengio, William W. Cohen, Ruslan Salakhutdinov, Christopher D. Manning
*These authors contributed equally. The order of authorship is decided through dice rolling.
We make HOTPOTQA publicly available at https://HotpotQA.github.io.
(Paper content header listing authors corresponds to Zhilin Yang, Peng Qi, Saizheng Zhang, Yoshua Bengio, William W. Cohen, Ruslan Salakhutdinov, Christopher D. Manning)

title: Yoshua Bengio: The Past, Present, and Future of Deep Learning
excerpts:
Section Title:  > Yoshua Bengio: The Past, Present, and Future of Deep Learning
Content:
Professor Bengio is a Full Professor at the [Université de Montréal](https://www.umontreal.ca/) as well as Founder and Scientific Director of the [MILA-Quebec AI Institute](https://mila.quebec/en/) and the [IVADO institute](https://ivado.ca/en/) . Best known for his work in pioneering deep learning, Bengio was [one of three awardees](https://awards.acm.org/about/2018-turing) of the 2018 A.M. Turing Award along with [Geoffrey Hinton](https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~hinton/) and [Yann LeCun](http://yann.lecun.com/) . He is also the awardee of the prestigious [Killam prize](https://canadacouncil.ca/funding/prizes/killam-prizes) and, as of this year, the computer scientist with the [highest h-index in the world](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=kukA0LcAAAAJ&hl=en) .

title: Yoshua Bengio
excerpts: **Yoshua Bengio** OC OQ OBE FRS FRSC (born March 5, 1964 <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> ) is a Canadian computer scientist , and a pioneer of artificial neural networks and deep learning . <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> He is a professor at the Université de Montréal and co-president and scientific director of the nonprofit LawZero. He founded Mila") , the Quebec Artificial Intelligence (AI) Institute, and was its scientific director until 2025.

title: Tech Brief on “AI Scientists: Safe and Useful AI?” Post by Yoshua Bengio | by Naveen Manwani | Medium
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > **Training an AI Scientist with Large Neural Nets for Bayesian Inference**
Content:
Till now every section talk about issues and risks, but in this section, Yoshua Bengio presents a solution for making Safe and Useful AI systems that would completely avoid the issue of setting goals and the concern of AI systems acting rogue in the Human world.
Press enter or click to view image in full size
Generated by Author
The solution is creating a **Large Neural Network Model** focused on building an understanding of what is observed (also known as data, in machine learning) and of theories that explain those observations, **Bayesian Inference** to be used to handle the uncertainty in the set of observations, these networks will be trained longer for guaranteed convergence towards Bayesian optimal answers.
